issue.description: The toobig_split chop reliably launches one split_file agent per oversized file every hour (unless the split_file hood is active): a deduped head proposal no longer cascades and kills the whole wait_on chain, once-per keys are released when their launches terminally fail, and agents whose completion markers were removed by dismissal are no longer misreported as failed.
issue.title: Restore toobig_split chop relaunches after the script-only chop migration

issue.description: 'Once-per key release in the Rust chop engine' section: add a release_chop_once_per engine operation to sase-core with a pyo3 binding and a thin axe_chop_facade wrapper so the runner can remove recorded once-per keys from the seen document.
issue.title: Once-per key release in the Rust chop engine

issue.description: 'Relink wait_on chains across dedupe-skipped proposals' section: change apply_chop_once_per so a proposal whose wait_on dependency was skipped as a once-per duplicate is relinked to its nearest accepted ancestor instead of being skipped, with launch and preview plumbing for the remapped waits.
issue.title: Relink wait_on chains across dedupe-skipped proposals

issue.description: 'Release once-per keys for terminally failed launches' section: record each launch's dedupe key in the durable launch descriptors and release keys through the new engine operation when the launch phase throws or when finalize_launched_chop_runs marks an agent's completion as failed.
issue.title: Release once-per keys for terminally failed launches

issue.description: 'Consult dismissed bundles before fail-closing chop completions' section: make the chop lifecycle's _agent_completion fall back to the dismissed-bundle archive when done.json is missing, so dismissed successful agents finalize as action_succeeded.
issue.title: Consult dismissed bundles before fail-closing chop completions

issue.description: 'Stale-state recovery and end-to-end verification' section: clear the poisoned toobig_split seen state on the host, exercise a real chop run end-to-end, and confirm agents launch for currently oversized files while inhibit_if and dedupe still behave correctly.
issue.title: Stale-state recovery and end-to-end verification

fields.notes: Implemented exact-artifact-timestamp dismissed-bundle fallback in chop lifecycle: top-level DONE bundles now prove success; FAILED, KILLED, unknown, and missing bundles remain fail-closed. Added root-versus-step and failure-state coverage. Focused lifecycle tests: 5 passed. just check formatting, lint, mypy, symvision, validation passed; full pytest had unrelated concurrent-suite failures, and serial rerun left only the pre-existing stale-lock test patching removed _git_lock_retry_delays.

Implemented transitive once-per wait_on relinking with effective wait mappings carried through proposal previews and launch dataclasses. Added policy coverage for duplicate chain heads, middle links, consecutive duplicates, ID/index references, and no surviving ancestor, plus runner coverage for emitted %wait directives. Focused suites: 18 passed; new tests after formatting: 4 passed. just check cleared all non-test gates; full tests reached 19,271 passed/7 skipped with four unrelated failures, two of which passed on rerun; persistent baseline failures were an obsolete _git_lock_retry_delays test patch target and a 107-pixel TUI golden drift.

Implemented release_chop_once_per in sase-core with typed wire request/result, shared seen-document validation, exact/idempotent key removal, PyO3 export, Python facade, and locked atomic seen-store persistence helper. Added Rust engine/schema tests and Python facade/policy contract coverage. Verification: focused Python tests passed (14); sase-core cargo fmt, clippy -D warnings, and full workspace tests passed. SASE just check passed all format/lint/validation stages and 19,271 tests; its full test lane has two unrelated reproducible baseline failures in the stale Git-lock mock and epic clan PNG snapshot.

Implemented durable effective dedupe keys and artifact timestamps in launch descriptors; partial launch failures now retain successful launch keys and release only accepted unlaunched keys; terminal lifecycle failures match descriptors by artifact timestamp then PID, release only failed-agent keys, tolerate legacy missing keys, and log release errors without aborting finalization. Added regression coverage for partial launches, mixed success/failure, successful-key retention, follow-up re-acceptance, legacy descriptors, release-error logging, and incomplete linkage. Verification: 17 focused tests passed; formatting, Ruff, mypy, Symvision, SASE validation, and committed-plan validation passed. Full parallel just check reached 19295 passed with 3 unrelated tests that pass in isolation; one-worker run reached 18915 passed before unrelated process-state leakage caused an OSError cascade.

Host recovery and live verification completed 2026-07-19. Updated the dev uv-tool runtime to sase 09f9151b6 and rebuilt sase-core-rs from c1f46f1. Initial relink evidence: scheduled run 20260719T135609_016224 evaluated 22 proposals, skipped genuine duplicates, relinked transitive waits, and launched 12 agents. Fixed-runtime failure/retry evidence: run 20260719T151858_469614 persisted dedupe keys for 4 launches; after terminal failures its lifecycle logged Released 4 once-per key(s), and dry run 20260719T153037_125973 accepted all 4 keys again with relinked waits. Removed the poisoned 64-entry seen.json. Clean real run 20260719T153143_284346 then accepted all 21 currently oversized files, launched 21 agents, persisted 21 unique keys, and produced a complete serialized wait chain. Immediate CLI rerun was blocked as already running; direct preflight returned skip: inhibited by active agent split_file.tests.test_vcs_log_resolve.a9029207-1 in hood split_file. sase axe chop doctor status: OK (all checks). Parent epic sase-7i intentionally left open.
